# Transcrição: 01 Calculating Required Ad Spend

- **Fonte:** 01 Calculating Required Ad Spend
- **Duração:** 10:42
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:04]

Okay, a really important step to making sure you're A, setting the rate expectations with your client and also knowing if you're on track or how much money they should spend, if it's even viable for them to be successful or to sell their product or offer on Facebook, is to actually understand what is going to be required to make this work for them. How many leads do they need? How many clicks do they need on their website? How much ad spend do they need to spend to get the revenue or the targets that they're after? So this is what we call working backwards. Now just to heads up everyone, there is a full suite of calculators below this training video which you have access to. And those calculators are pre-populated and you can just punch in the numbers that your client is telling you and it will show you the ad spends. Now bear in mind those are averages for certain industries and verticals. You obviously need to consider any nuances or quirks specific to any client that you have. So I'm going to show you an example of how you might work backwards if you don't have the calculators. So you don't really need the calculators, you can do this yourself. And this is an example of how we would do this. So what I'm going to do is I'm going to create like a made up business and let's say that I'm trying to get 400 people purchasing a... Well let's say it's an online course, okay? So we're going to go purchase and I want 400. So I've created this really simple graph here, you guys can do this, no excuses. And essentially this is the last step of my funnel, right? I want people to purchase from me. So what is the step before someone purchases? Well the step before they can purchase is they're going to be hitting the landing page, the sales page, right? So let's just put this here. And let's say our conversion rate here is 5%. Now if you're not sure about certain specific conversion rates, there are some benchmarks that I outlined for you and feel free to ask as well. Different industries will convert at different levels, free offers will convert at different levels than a $1,000 product for example. But in this instance, let's say our landing page converts at 5%. Now let me pull up my calculator because what I'm going to show you here is quite simple. I'm just going to go, I need 400 people divided by .05 equals 8,000. So I need 8,000 hits to sales page, okay? Now even just with this, you could almost literally, technically speaking, calculate your ad spend from here because you could go, well I know that on average it's going to cost me about $2 to $3 per click. So my ad spend is going to be between $18 to $24,000.

## [03:04 - 06:06]

But let's take it a step further still, okay? So what is the step before them hitting the sales page? Now depending on what funnel you're running here, it might actually be an opt-in. Maybe you've got a lead magnet that people are going to be opting into. Perhaps even it's simply a video or an ad that you're retargeting. So let's say we've got an ad here that's sending them to the sales page and this is going to be an ad retargeting people. So it's going to be middle of funnel ad. And our click through rate here is going to be 3%. It's going to be a bit higher than normal. So what I can do here is I can actually start to deduce the audience size that I'm actually going to need for the top of funnel. Now this is really important because sometimes we get asked a lot of questions like how big should my audience size be? Well really, there's no right or wrong. Technically speaking, it should be as big as you need it to be to hit your target. So this is a great way to calculate this. So if we need 8,000 hits and our click through rate is 3%, we go 8,000 divided by .03. We need an audience size of 266,666,000 people. So we're reach I should say. So our audience size should be at the very least this big, right? Now you can even take this a step further. So you can go, you can calculate the CPM and what ad spend will be required from here. Or you can even go, okay, if I need 200,000 people in my retargeting audience in order to drive 8,000 clicks in order to get 400 new customers. And I'm using a video top of funnel. So let's say it's a video. And I know that my 10 second video views cost about 0.7 cents, yeah? For 10 seconds. If you're doing 3 seconds, that might be 1 cent or it might even be like this. But let's say it's 7 cents for a 10 second video view because we want to make sure they're still engaged somewhat. I'll go 266 times 0.07. And my ad spend is boom, $18,000. Not far off from what we had sort of estimated when we were at this stage, right? 18 to 24,000. So according to this, if I can hit these stats, my required ad spend is going to be $18,666 in order to make 400 purchases or sales. So this is one way that you would work backwards. Now, clearly, there's a couple points here.

## [06:06 - 09:08]

You need to know the funnel that you're running with, right? You need to know the benchmarks or industry conversion rates. Now let's do another example. Let's say that you are working with a lawyer. So they're after clients ultimately, right? Even though you may not be responsible for this step, it's really good to be able to map out to them and show them, okay, so if you need this many clients, this is how many leads or consults I need to send your way. So let's say that he says to you, I want five clients a month, okay? So that's the ultimate target. Now when you're setting expectations with your client, you're going to be hopefully telling them, hey, look, I know in the past you were relying on word of mouth and referrals and that type of traffic converts at 90%, but with Facebook ads, your conversion rate is probably going to be more along 10%. So let's say he converts 10% of the leads that you send his way into clients. Now clients for him, bear in mind, are going to have really high lifetime value in this industry. They're worth at least a few thousand dollars if not more and the lifetime value in that industry is very, very high. So the next step above getting a client is to actually book a consult for your client. So getting people to book a time in with the lawyer and on the phone, the lawyer will talk to them and convert them into a client. So let's say five clients is the goal divided by 0.10. Our goal is to send 50 leads to your 50 leads slash consults to your client. So you can almost calculate an average ad spend just based on this. So if you know, for example, that the average cost per lead for this industry without a hook or without a juicy hook is going to be anywhere between 50 to 100 dollars, well you could just go 50 times 50. So setting the expectation that ad spend needs to be between two and a half to five thousand dollars per month in order for him to land five clients. So that's one way to do it, but let's actually take this a step back and show you why it might cost 50 to 100 dollars so that you fully understand why that might be and also explaining this to your client. So the step before the free consult, let's pretend it's a lead ad. It could even be a landing page at this stage, okay? And let's say that this converts at 10 percent as well. So we need 50 leads divided by 0.10. We need 500 clicks, if you will, on lead ad. Now another way that you could calculate the ad spend from here is go, well what's an average cost per click?

## [09:08 - 10:42]

And for example, if you're saying the average cost per click is going to be about five dollars for this particular industry, remember lawyers are usually restricted geographically, so cost per clicks are sometimes a bit higher here and also it's a bit of a lower affinity type of industry. So you will find your cost per clicks are a little bit higher here. Again, so this is a two and a half thousand dollar ad spend that it's telling us right here. So we could calculate it based off this as well. Now another way to do that is to take it again a step further and go, well what is going to be the average click-through rate? Let's say it's one percent because that's what we want to aim for. So we'll do 500 divided by 0.01. So we need an audience size, we need to reach at least 50,000 people. So what you could do here again is calculate with your average CPM to also get a total estimated required ad spend. But essentially what you'd be looking at is almost two and a half thousand dollars to generate five clients. So we go two and a half thousand divided by 50 and so you'd be looking at paying about fifty dollars per lead in this instance. So anywhere between fifty to a hundred dollars per lead. And this is how you would work backwards. Obviously again you need to know the funnel and you need to know the benchmarks and industry. But if you're not sure just ask and I can help you with this.
